compressed_view: Complete v2 refactor methodology across all phases: 9-phase sequence (Research through Docs) required for v2_refactor work type. Cross-skill pipeline: /qa audit produces findings, /ux produces decisions + 9-section spec + compliance gateway 5/5, /design governs tokens, /dev builds bottom-up in 4 task layers (Foundation parallel, Main Assembly, Post-Assembly, Verification), /qa tests with CI gate. Timeline: 8-9 days for complex screens (17 tasks), 4-5 day critical path with parallelism. PR structure: foundation layer commit, v2 view assembly, a11y pass, pbxproj swap, tests. High-risk files (DomainModels, EncryptionService, SyncServices, AuthManager, AIOrchestrator) require extra review flag. Metrics mandatory: primary metric with baseline/target, guardrails (crash-free >99.5%, cold start <2s, sync >99%, CI >95%), kill criteria. Cache-accelerated refactors: v4.1 screens completed in 1-2h vs 5-36h earlier (55-70% cache hit rates). Deferred features spin off as separate PM cycles.

task_signature.context: Complete v2 refactor methodology spanning all phases. Used by /design (Phase 3 compliance), /dev (Phase 4 implementation), /qa (Phase 5 testing). Synthesizes the Home v2 (PR #61) and Training v2 (PR #74) execution patterns.

learned_patterns.pattern: Phase sequence for v2 refactors — all 9 phases required
learned_patterns.decision: No phase skipped for a v2_refactor work_type. Phase sequence: Research (audit report + decisions log) → PRD (scope + analytics spec + metrics + kill criteria) → Tasks (task breakdown with critical path) → UX (ux-research.md + ux-spec.md + compliance gateway + build prompts) → Implement (bottom-up build) → Test (CI gate + test suite) → Review (parallel diff) → Merge (PR) → Docs. Each transition requires explicit user approval recorded in state.json.

typical_timeline.training_screen_complexity: 8-9 days estimate (16 tasks, decomposed to 7 files)
typical_timeline.critical_path: 4-5 days with parallelism in foundation layer tasks

learned_patterns.pattern: Audit → Decision → Spec → Build pipeline (cross-skill flow)
learned_patterns.decision: The core pipeline is: /qa audit → /ux decisions + spec → /design tokens → /dev build → /qa test. Each hand-off has a deliverable: audit report (findings P0/P1/P2 with tractability tags) → decisions log (user-approved decisions on each 'needs-decision' finding) → ux-spec.md (9 sections, compliance gateway 5/5) → build prompts → implementation → test report.

cross_skill_handoffs.research_to_prd: Deliverable: v2-audit-report.md with Decisions Log. All 'needs-decision' items resolved by user.
cross_skill_handoffs.prd_to_tasks: Deliverable: prd.md with analytics spec, success metrics, kill criteria, acceptance criteria (35+ for complex features).
cross_skill_handoffs.tasks_to_ux: Deliverable: tasks.md with critical path analysis, parallel foundation layer tasks identified.
cross_skill_handoffs.ux_to_implement: Deliverables: ux-research.md, ux-spec.md (9 sections), ux-build.md prompt, design-build.md prompt.
cross_skill_handoffs.implement_to_test: Deliverables: v2 Swift file(s), pbxproj updated, v1 marked HISTORICAL, checklist.completed = true.
cross_skill_handoffs.test_to_review: Deliverables: CI green on feature branch, test counts in state.json.
cross_skill_handoffs.review_to_merge: Deliverable: PR created, parallel diff (feature vs main), high-risk file check.

learned_patterns.pattern: High-risk file identification — extra review required
learned_patterns.decision: These files require extra review when touched: DomainModels.swift, EncryptionService.swift, SupabaseSyncService.swift, CloudKitSyncService.swift, SignInService.swift, AuthManager.swift, AIOrchestrator.swift. Home v2 and Training v2 both had zero high-risk files touched (pure UI + service layer work). If a v2 refactor requires touching high-risk files, flag explicitly in Phase 7 (Review).
source_executions: home-today-screen state.json review.risks = ['zero high-risk files touched']

learned_patterns.pattern: Task layer architecture for v2 refactors
learned_patterns.decision: Tasks organized in 4 layers with explicit dependency management. Layer 1 (Foundation — all parallel): token additions (T1-T3), new components (T4-T5), service classes (T6), analytics enums (T7), supporting views (T9-T10). Layer 2 (Main Assembly — depends on all Layer 1): T8 main view file. Layer 3 (Post-Assembly): T11 a11y pass, T12 pbxproj swap, T13 v1 historical marker. Layer 4 (Verification): T14 checklist walk, T15-T17 test authoring.

task_count_patterns.complex_screen_v2: 16-20 tasks (Training v2: 16 but decomposed to 7 files), 4-5 day critical path

source_executions: home-today-screen state.json tasks T1-T17, 17 tasks in 4 layers
home-today-screen tasks note: 'Critical path ~5 days with parallelism'

learned_patterns.decision: v2 refactors ship as single PRs with all commits squashed or kept as logical units. Home v2 PR #61: 5 commits (foundation layer, v2 MainScreenView 703 lines, a11y pass, pbxproj swap, tests). Training v2 PR #74: container + 6 extracted views + 12 analytics events + 16 tests. PR description must list: lines added, components promoted, tokens added, test count, analytics events added.
commit_sequence: 2. Main v2 view file (the assembly — typically the largest commit)

learned_patterns.pattern: Deferred features from v2 refactor — sub-feature spin-off process
learned_patterns.decision: Some v1 gaps are too large or complex to fix in the v2 pass. These are spun off as separate PM cycles with their own PRDs. Home v2 deferred: Status+Goal merged card, Metric Tile Deep Linking, home_metric_tile_tap analytics event. Training v2 deferred: Rest day positive experience redesign, Advanced AI engine exercise recommendations. Deferred items recorded in state.json prd.non_scope and research.deferred_features.

learned_patterns.pattern: Metrics and kill criteria — required for every v2 refactor
learned_patterns.decision: state.json phases.metrics must have: primary metric (with baseline + target), secondary metrics array, guardrails (crash_free_rate > 99.5%, cold_start < 2s, sync_success_rate > 99%, ci_pass_rate > 95%), first_review_date, kill_criteria. Home v2 primary metric: sessions_per_day (target 1.5). Training v2 primary: training_set_completed (weekly/30d/90d). Kill criteria must be quantitative.

v4.1 refactors (Nutrition, Stats, Settings) completed in 1-2h each vs 5-36h for earlier screens. L2 cache hits on ux-foundations-map and design-system-decisions eliminated research phase re-derivation. Cache hit rates: Nutrition ~55%, Stats ~65%, Settings ~70%.
source_executions: nutrition-v2 state.json: research.sources includes 'L2 cache ux-foundations-map'
stats-v2 state.json: research.sources includes 'L2 cache ux-foundations-map' + 'L1 cache design/ux-foundations-application'
settings-v2 state.json: research.sources includes 'L2 cache' + 'screen-audits-apr9 memory'

anti_patterns.pattern: Treating v2 refactor as a simple 'cleanup' with no metrics or kill criteria
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: v2 refactors are full 9-phase features. They require PRD with success metrics, kill criteria, and post-launch review cadence. 'It's just a refactor' is not a valid reason to skip metrics — every refactor changes user-facing behavior and must be measurable.
anti_patterns.source_execution: CLAUDE.md non-negotiable rule 2: No PRD without success metrics

anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Home v2 Phase 3 took from 12:00 to 13:00 (one hour). Training v2 Phase 3 produced ux-spec with schematics (low + high fidelity). Starting implementation before the spec is approved means building against an unvalidated design — expensive to redo.
anti_patterns.source_execution: CLAUDE.md non-negotiable rule 1: No phase is skipped. home-today-screen transitions: ux_or_integration approved_at before implementation started_at

anti_patterns.pattern: Merging feature branch before main CI is also green
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: CI must pass on BOTH the feature branch AND main. A green feature branch that merges into a red main causes a broken build state. Home v2 review: ci_main = true AND ci_feature = true before PR #61 was merged.
anti_patterns.source_execution: home-today-screen state.json review.ci_main = true, ci_feature = true

speedup_instructions: This is the master cross-skill reference for any v2 refactor. /design reads the token + component patterns. /dev reads the task layer architecture and commit sequence. /qa reads the test strategy embedded in the phase sequence. Start here when kicking off a new screen refactor — the Phase sequence, timeline estimates, and PR structure patterns all encode the lessons from Home v2 and Training v2 so the third and subsequent refactors are faster.
